topic: HL Regional Option: History of the Americas
subject: history

items.q: Did the Emancipation Proclamation free all enslaved people in the USA?
items.correct: No - it applied to areas in rebellion, where Lincoln had no immediate control; slavery was abolished nationally by the 13th Amendment in 1865.
items.misconception: Lincoln's Emancipation Proclamation immediately freed every enslaved person in America.
items.random_wrong: The Emancipation Proclamation was a law passed by the Confederacy.
items.why: The Proclamation's symbolic fame outruns its legal scope; distinguishing it from the 13th Amendment is essential for accurate analysis.

items.q: Was the Cuban Missile Crisis resolved by an American military strike?
items.correct: No - it ended through a naval quarantine and negotiation: the USSR withdrew its missiles in return for a US non-invasion pledge and the secret removal of US missiles from Turkey.
items.misconception: The USA bombed the missile sites in Cuba to end the crisis.
items.random_wrong: Cuba voluntarily left the Soviet alliance to end the crisis.
items.why: The crisis is remembered as a showdown won by force; in fact mutual concessions, one of them secret, defused it.

items.q: Did Latin American independence bring immediate democracy and stability?
items.correct: No - most new states experienced decades of caudillo strongman rule, civil conflict and continued exclusion of indigenous and formerly enslaved peoples.
items.misconception: After independence, Latin American countries became stable democracies like the USA.
items.random_wrong: Latin America remained entirely under Spanish rule until 1900.
items.why: Students map the US story onto the whole hemisphere; the contrast in post-independence paths is a core IB comparison.

items.q: Who led the Cuban Revolution that overthrew Batista in 1959?
items.why: Correct answer: Fidel Castro. “Juan Peron” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What were the main results of the US Civil War?
items.correct: The Union was preserved and slavery was abolished
items.random_wrong: The USA lost territory to Mexico
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The Union was preserved and slavery was abolished. “The Confederacy gained independence” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which president launched the New Deal in response to the Depression?
items.why: Correct answer: Franklin D. Roosevelt. “Herbert Hoover” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Simon Bolivar is best known for what?
items.correct: Leading independence campaigns in northern South America
items.why: It's Leading independence campaigns in northern South America. “Ruling Brazil as emperor” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year did the US Civil War end?
items.why: Exact answer: 1865 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
